density_note.verdict: ZERO fair-use TV/news soundbite clips selected. Deliberate, rights-safe call. The two beats that were reserved for candidate soundbites (context-01, matters-01) are recut to Remotion TextSlate big-word cards.
rationale: Thesis is 'the friendly PAC NAME is the costume' — a money/disclosure story about super-PACs and their hidden donors, NOT about what any candidate said. No beat's meaning depends on a candidate's quote or moving face.

Attaching real candidate floor/debate/announcement footage (Paxton, Allred, Crockett) to a nonpartisan money-disguise verdict risks a partisan read the script's both-sides framing (GOP-aimed AND Dem-aimed money) is built to avoid. Same call made on sealed-aipac-campus-eo for the same reason.

Verifiable real sources DO exist (yt-dlp probe confirmed: FOX4 DFW 'Rep. Jasmine Crockett announces run for U.S. Senate' W-Pe1GifYLs; 'Texas Senate Debate' pPeyyhHfcus; LiveNOW/FOX 'Trump endorses Paxton' ss3DyFqxgew). They were NOT cut because (a) precise ≤6s lip-matched in/out windows could not be frame-verified within budget for all three, and (b) per doctrine, an unverified in/out point risks a mismatched-speaker defect at the never-overridable Stage 25/26 QC. Better to ship clean Remotion than a partially-verified clip.

Named donors and candidates that the script DOES name are carried by politician-caricature (book-style illustration, not a real photo or AI face) per the validated CR Bush/Massie/Rabb pattern: john-nau, stephen-schwarzman, ken-paxton, jeff-yass, colin-allred, reid-hoffman, jasmine-crockett. All readable money/claims are Remotion (CountUp, MoneyFlow, ChartBar, DonorComparison, SourceCard, TextSlate, VerdictStamp).
